pattern_description: BROKEN DAG (SPURIOUS EDGE): Identical to DAG #9 with one extra edge added (Visit to Asia → Lung Cancer). Tests whether the LLM can identify a causal claim that is not supported by domain knowledge — visiting Asia does not directly cause lung cancer.
difficulty_notes: Critique task. The flaw is an unjustified edge. The model must recognize that while the rest of the graph is plausible, this particular claim is unsupported and should explain why.
description: A modified version of the Asia network with a spurious edge added: this graph incorrectly claims that visiting Asia directly causes Lung Cancer.

explanation_points: This graph contains a spurious causal edge
Visiting Asia is associated with tuberculosis exposure but is not a known direct cause of lung cancer

counterfactual.intervention: Not applicable — the graph contains a structural error
counterfactual.expected_outcome: Any counterfactual involving travel to Asia would overestimate the effect on lung cancer because of the spurious edge

flaws.explanation: There is no established causal mechanism linking travel to Asia directly to lung cancer. The original Asia DAG includes this travel variable specifically as a tuberculosis risk factor, not a cancer risk factor. This added edge has no biological justification.
